residual_risks: No CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md exists anywhere in this repository (verified via recursive search from repo root through every changed file's ancestor directories: repo root, engine/, analytics/, config/, docs/, ops/, skills/trader/, tests/). There is also no CONTRIBUTING.md, .cursorrules, or equivalent convention file, and README.md is a one-line stub. This diff is the repo's first code (the base commit ce7a260 contains only README.md and two docs files), so there are no pre-existing in-repo conventions to compare new files against either. As a result, no citable project-standards violations can be reported for this diff -- there is nothing written down to violate.

skills/trader/SKILL.md's frontmatter (name: trader; description: 'Operate the i-am-not-a-trader-bot trading system...') was checked against /Users/robertwarren/nanoclaw/CONTRIBUTING.md's 'SKILL.md format' section (name lowercase/alphanumeric+hyphens/<=64 chars, description required, file <500 lines) since the orchestrator asked about the rep-update/SKILL.md pattern it claims to mirror. It appears consistent with that external pattern (146 lines, valid name, descriptive frontmatter). However, that CONTRIBUTING.md lives in a different repository (nanoclaw) and is not an ancestor-directory standards file for i-am-not-a-trader-bot, so this is informational only and is not reported as a finding -- it fails the evidence requirement of citing a rule from a standards file that governs this repo.

Because no standards files exist, this review could not check: frontmatter completeness rules, reference-inclusion conventions (markdown links vs backtick paths vs @-inclusion), cross-reference/agent-name qualification rules, cross-platform tool-portability rules, shell-vs-native tool selection policy, or writing-style rules (second person, hedge words) for this diff. If the project intends any such conventions (e.g., for skills/trader/SKILL.md or docs/nanoclaw-setup.md), they are currently unenforceable because they are not written down anywhere a reviewer or agent could discover them.

Recommend the project add a root CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md (and optionally a docs/ or skills/ scoped one) capturing at least: Python style expectations already implicit in pyproject.toml's ruff config (line-length 100, target py312, E/F/I/UP/B rules), the SKILL.md frontmatter/format expectations skills/trader/SKILL.md is clearly written to satisfy, and any host-vs-container path conventions used throughout docs/nanoclaw-setup.md and skills/trader/SKILL.md (e.g. /workspace/extra/trader-bot vs /workspace/extra/trader-runtime). Without this, future diffs in this repo are equally unauditable against project-specific rules.
